title: Fixing CSS Grid Layout with Inconsistent Row Height Calculation

statement.markdown: Given a grid layout with variable row heights, implement a function `calculateRowHeights` that takes an array of integers representing the heights of each row and returns an array of integers representing the correct row heights with consistent calculations.
statement.inputFormat: Array of integers representing row heights
statement.outputFormat: Array of integers representing correct row heights
statement.notes: The function should handle edge cases such as empty arrays or arrays with single element.

constraints.description: Number of rows in the grid

examples.explanation: The heights are calculated by adding the height of the current row to the height of the previous row and then subtracting the height of the previous row from the total height of the current row.
For a single row, the height is the same as the input height.
For an empty array, the output is an empty array.

hints.text: Use a for loop to iterate over the array of row heights.

editorial.title: Editorial Solution
editorial.intuition: The function should calculate the correct row heights by adding the height of the current row to the height of the previous row.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve using a for loop to calculate the correct row heights.
editorial.betterSolution: A more efficient solution would involve using a for loop to calculate the correct row heights and storing the result in an array.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a for loop to calculate the correct row heights and storing the result in an array, with a time complexity of O(n) where n is the number of rows.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the function calculates the correct row heights for all possible inputs.
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Forgetting to handle edge cases
Mistake 2: Incorrectly calculating the row heights
